description: Shared inbound-verification helper (Bot Framework JWT verify + audience check + trusted serviceUrl allow-list) extracted from the bot route and reusable by all Teams inbound surfaces
Message-extension query endpoint verifies Bot Framework JWT before invoking the handler; unverified requests rejected 401
Quick-actions endpoint verifies Bot Framework JWT before invoking the handler; unverified requests rejected 401
Bot messages endpoint fails closed: when TEAMS_BOT_APP_* credentials are unset, inbound activities are rejected (401/403) instead of processed unauthenticated
Caller identity (Microsoft tenant id, AAD object id) sourced from verified JWT claims; mismatches with the activity body are rejected
Diagnostics check verifying generated-manifest botId/webApplicationInfo.id consistency with runtime TEAMS_BOT_APP_ID (registration-mismatch footgun surfaced)
Production notification path delivers through the EE implementation that records teams_notification_deliveries rows (skipped/delivered/failed) for every attempt
Duplicate delivery module in packages/notifications reduced to a CE-safe dynamic delegator to the EE implementation (no duplicated delivery logic)
Retryable Graph notification failures (429 with Retry-After, 5xx) retried with bounded backoff before a failed delivery row is recorded
Diagnostics recent_delivery_health step reflects real production delivery rows end-to-end
Approval-path Teams meeting creation includes the client contact as a required attendee on the Graph event
Approval-path Teams meeting creation includes the assigned technician as a required attendee on the Graph event
Attendees receive native Outlook/Teams calendar invites for generated meetings (Graph event created with attendee list, invites dispatched)
Admin toggle 'Send calendar invites to participants' on Teams meeting settings, default on, respected by both meeting-creation entry points
Generated meeting subject and body carry appointment context (service/appointment title and PSA link)
Reschedule PATCHes subject and attendees in addition to start/end (attendee set refreshed when the assignee changed)
Rescheduling an approved appointment sends updated calendar invites to attendees via the Graph event update
Declining a previously-approved appointment request deletes the associated Graph meeting (parity with cancel)
Cancel/decline Graph meeting cleanup runs as an idempotent retried job via IJobRunner; online_meetings shows cancel_pending until cleanup confirmed
Client-portal cancel dialogs surface the existing teamsMeetingWarning (localized) before confirmation
Teams meeting CRUD returns typed results distinguishing created/failed(reason)/skipped(not_configured|addon_inactive); callers no longer conflate them
Approver UI surfaces meeting-generation failure at approval time with explicit 'approve without meeting' and 'retry' choices (inline alert, not toast)
'Generate Teams meeting' retry action available on approved appointment requests that lack a meeting link
Failed meeting creation persists an online_meetings row with status='failed' and an error code (no silent absence)
// @ts-nocheck removed from appointmentRequestManagementActions.ts and the file passes typecheck
Artifact change-notification subscriptions ensured at meeting-creation time, not only via the 30-minute renewal schedule
Artifact subscription renewal scheduled through the IJobRunner abstraction so it runs on both Temporal and pg-boss deployments
Polling fallback job sweeps recording_pending meetings after meeting end (using listPendingRecordings) and fetches artifacts independent of webhooks
recording_fetch_attempts hard cap replaced with a bounded backoff schedule; early empty notifications do not drive a meeting to terminal no_recording
Diagnostics checks for artifact subscription expiry and webhook base-URL reachability
Unlinked bot users receive a sign-in card with a working sign-in button (deep link into the Microsoft account-link flow) instead of a dead-end text card
Completing sign-in from the bot links the account and the bot sends a welcome/confirmation card
Bot accepts human ticket references: ticket number with or without '#' resolves to the ticket
'ticket <text>' falls back to title search returning a numbered pick list when the argument is not an id/number
List results (my tickets, my approvals, search) are numbered and follow-up commands accept ordinal references (e.g. 'approve 2', 'ticket 3')
Per-conversation context of last-listed entities persisted (tenant-keyed, TTL) to back ordinal references
Commands with missing arguments prompt with concrete examples instead of the generic unsupported fallback
Near-miss command suggestions for misspelled commands ('assing ticket…' → did you mean 'assign ticket')
'new ticket <title>' (optional client) creates a ticket from bot chat via the create_ticket_from_message registry action
Bot replies migrated to Adaptive Cards with hero-card fallback for clients that reject them
Ticket cards carry inline actions (Assign to me, Add note, Open) that execute via the action registry and update the card in place
Manifest bot command list generated from the same source of truth as the handler (includes my approvals, approval commands, new ticket)
Unsupported-scope (channel) messages get a friendly reply explaining supported scopes with a docs link
Optional bot-DM delivery of notifications as actionable Adaptive Cards using stored conversation references
Per-category notification channel preference (activity feed / bot DM / both) configurable in Teams admin settings
Help card generated from the action registry filtered by the requesting user's RBAC (only shows commands they can run)
Teams settings shows a prominent banner when bot connector credentials are missing ('bot cannot reply'), linking to diagnostics
LLM intent parsing maps free-text bot messages to existing registry actions; deterministic parser remains default/fallback
Every NL-parsed mutation shows a confirmation card before executing
NL execution goes exclusively through executeTeamsAction (RBAC, audit, idempotency preserved; no scope widening via prompt injection)
NL disambiguation dialog when the target entity is ambiguous
NL layer gated behind AI Assistant add-on, a tenant toggle, and its own PostHog flag
Guided setup wizard stepper: profile → Graph permissions → bot registration → activate → package → sideload instructions → verify
Live Graph credential validation: token acquisition attempted on save/validate (not just field-presence readiness)
Per-permission Graph consent probe reporting granted/missing status for each required application permission
On-demand Bot Framework token acquisition validation step in wizard/diagnostics

Complete admin runbook docs/integrations/teams-setup.md covering Azure app registration, Bot Framework registration (and its relation to platform creds + manifest botId), Graph permissions, access policies, sideload, verification
In-app links from wizard/settings steps to the corresponding runbook sections
Stale-manifest detection: warning when deployment base URL or selected profile changed after the last package generation
Delivery log viewer in Teams settings consuming listTeamsDeliveries with status/category filters and pagination
Audit log viewer in Teams settings consuming listTeamsAuditEvents with surface/action filters and pagination
Troubleshooting panel mapping delivery error codes (graph_unauthorized, user_not_mapped, addon_inactive, package_misconfigured, …) to concrete remedies
Centralized tenantHasTeamsAddOn/assertTeamsAddOn helper exported from one module; all duplicated copies migrated to it
Teams settings paywall card shows included capabilities and a purchase CTA deep-linking billing admins to Account Management add-on purchase
Expired Teams add-on soft-disables: sends skipped with addon_inactive, configuration and history preserved, admin banner explains state
ADD_ON_DESCRIPTIONS[TEAMS] updated to include meetings and recordings/transcripts capture
